{{Quote|Conveniently, as these things happen; we've made one for the Rebel Alliance and one for the Empire. Of course, we took precautions… so that each wouldn't know what we're developing for the other side.|Anonymous [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] member|<ref name="INMW" />}}
The '''Crusader Mark III armor''' was a model of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] developed by the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] for use by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Description==
{{Quote|With the recent advances we've made at the Death Watch installation working on our Mandalorian technology, we've been able to create two new series of armor.|Anonymous Black Sun member|<ref name="INMW" />}}
The [[Crusader armor]]s [[Crusader Mark II armor|Mark II]] and Mark III were the two versions of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] Crusader armors developed by the Black Sun. They shared similar technical characteristics but different appearances.<ref name="INMW" /> The Mark III model resembled [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusader]] [[Neo-Crusader armor|light armor]] developed about 4000 years prior.<ref name="KotORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Crusader armors offered protection against kinetic damage as well as from energy-based weapons like [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. A typical complete armor set comprised a helmet, a chest plate, left & right biceps, left & right bracers, gloves, a belt, leggings and boots. Crusader armors came in a wide variety of colors.<ref name="SWG:TBoR" />

==History==
{{Quote|Why should it matter that we supply armor to both sides? They don't know … they both think that they have an exclusive deal with us, riiiiight! We're the Black Sun.|Anonymous Black Sun member|<ref name="INMW" />}}
[[File:CrusaderMarkIIIRestuss.jpg|thumb|left|220px|An Imperial wearing Crusader Mark III armor during the Battle of Restuss.]]
After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the members of the criminal organization [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] realized that thanks to their fruitful collaboration with the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]], a unique business opportunity presented itself. Due to advances in their armor forges working on Mandalorian technology within the [[Death Watch bunker|Death Watch Bunker]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]], the Black Sun realized it could create and distribute different types of battle armor to both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] by ensuring each armor type appeared distinctly different. This positively impacted Black Sun through increased credit flow and freedom from both factions due to the raging Galactic Civil War.<ref name="INMW" />

[[File:CrusaderMarkIIIRestuss2.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Imperial troops wearing Crusader Mark III armors on Restuss.]]
Crusader armors were introduced around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]<ref name="SWG_timeline" /> and the Mark III model was distributed to the Galactic Empire. It was first seen in action on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo's]] moon of [[Rori/Legends|Rori]] during the [[Battle of Restuss]]. The armor was worn by several [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] such as [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Roth (Zabrak)|Roth]] and [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Wholding]]. In addition, a number of Crusader MK III series was distributed by the Empire during all along the Battle of Restuss. Imperial [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] who took part in the battle were awarded with battle [[commendation]]s, which could then be traded to Imperial [[commendation officer]]s in exchange for items such as the MK III armor or various other weaponry.<ref name="SWG:TBoR" />

Later the same year, [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] wore a Crusader Mark III armor during the [[Empire Day/Legends|Empire Day]] festivities on Naboo.<ref name="SWG:AMJ" /> An Imperial Crusader armor was also acquired by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Berok Skoff]] of the [[Mos Espa Militia]] and used during the [[Tusken Raider Invasion of Mos Espa|Battle of Mos Espa]].<ref name="SWG:TA" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Crusader Mark III armor appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:TBoR" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Crusader armors were added to the game along with the "[[W:c:swg:Restuss|Battle of Restuss]]" [[Wikipedia:Player versus player|PvP]] battlefield,<ref name="SWG:TBoR" /> introduced in the "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 1.1|Chapter 1.1]]" released on [[June 27]], [[2006]].<ref name="SonyCite-Chapter Archive 2006">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64186&resource=publish|text=Chapter Archive 2006}}</ref>

As part of the Battle of Restuss story arc, players might receive commendation items for assisting their chosen faction during the battle, which could be stacked and traded for various items such as this armor, from Commendation Officer [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player characters]].<ref name="SWG:TBoR" />
